title: ST_558_HW_3
text:
#Task 1 library(tidyverse) library(readr) library(dplyr) #1 # “../MyData.csv”
#2 # R is good for all following task: # Read raw data in or connect to a database # Manipulate data as need # Subset # Create new variables # Summarize data to create meaningful insights # Modeling data to make inference or predict outcomes # Communicate our results via dashboards, documents, model files, etc.
#3 # Git is a version control software # Github is an online hosting service for Git-based projects
#4 # First, a tibble prints more nicely. It only shows the first few rows and columns that fit on the screen, # while a data.frame may print too much.

title: ST_558_HW_3
text:
Question 3
#1. Read in the White Wine Excel file
library(readxl) white_wine <- read_excel(“~/Desktop/ST 558/ST 558 HW3/white-wine.xlsx”, sheet = 1) white_wine
#2. Rename columns using Sheet 2
new_names <- read_excel(“~/Desktop/ST 558/ST 558 HW3/white-wine.xlsx”, sheet = 2)
colnames(white_wine) <- new_names[[1]]
#3. Add type column for white wine
library(dplyr)
white_wine <- white_wine |> mutate(type = “white”)
#4. Read in the Red Wine CSV
library(readr) red_wine <- read_delim(“~/Desktop/ST 558/ST 558 HW3/red-wine.csv”, delim = “;”, show_col_types = FALSE) colnames(red_wine) <- new_names[[1]]
red_wine <- red_wine |> mutate(type = “red”)
#5. Combine both datasets
wine <- dplyr::bind_rows(white_wine, red_wine)
#6-9. Combined data object to filter the data
wine |> # Filter high quality and reasonable alcohol filter(quality > 6.5 & alcohol < 132) |> # Sort highest quality to lowest arrange(desc(quality)) |> # Select acid columns, alcohol, type, quality dplyr::select(contains(“acid”), alcohol, type, quality) |> # Add mean and sd of alcohol for each quality level group_by(quality) |> mutate( mean_alcohol = mean(alcohol), sd_alcohol = sd(alcohol) )

title: ST_558_HW_3
text:
Question 4
#1. Connect to the database and list all tables
install.packages(“RSQLite”)
library(DBI)
con <- dbConnect(RSQLite::SQLite(), “~/Desktop/ST 558/ST 558 HW3/lahman.db”)
dbListTables(con)
#2. Using the tbl() function and dplyr to return all of the data
library(dbplyr)
tbl(con, “Teams”) |> filter(yearID == 2015 ) |> collect()
#3. Using the sql() function
tbl(con, sql( “SELECT * FROM Teams WHERE yearID == 2015”)) |> collect()
#4. Inducted players only
hof <- tbl(con, sql(” SELECT playerID, yearID, category From HallOfFame WHERE inducted = ‘Y’ “)) |> collect() hof
#5. Add nameFirst and nameLast from People
hof_names <- tbl(con, sql(” SELECT h.playerID, h.yearID, h.category, p.nameFirst, p.nameLast From HallOfFame h JOIN People p ON h.playerID = p.playerID WHERE h.inducted = ‘Y’ “)) |> collect() hof_names
#6. Managers win/loss records and win percentage
managers <- tbl(con, “Managers”) |> select(playerID, G, W, L) |> group_by(playerID) |> summarize(G_managed = sum(G, na.rm = TRUE), Total_W = sum(W, na.rm = TRUE), Total_L = sum(L, na.rm = TRUE)) |> collect() |> mutate(win_pct = Total_W/G_managed) |> arrange(desc(win_pct)) managers
#7. Join two previous table
hof_names |> inner_join(managers, by = “playerID”)
